possible_failure_mode: The system's summaries or hypotheses are interpreted as final diagnoses.
evidence_refs: WellBe Safety; Research F safety_risks; SHM R001
guardrail: Use memory/investigation language; ban 'you have X'; require safety gate and source links.

possible_failure_mode: User or clinician treats a normal test as closure while symptoms persist.
evidence_refs: Research F PI006/safety_risks; Research B normal-test trap
guardrail: Show test date/scope and unresolved symptom status; recommend follow-up discussion.

possible_failure_mode: Too many reminders/alerts cause important items to be ignored.
evidence_refs: Research D alert overload; Research F PI024; SHM R002
guardrail: Prioritize open loops by risk/time; suppress duplicates; user notification controls; measure false positives.

possible_failure_mode: The app implies an owner or promise that the health system has not actually accepted.
evidence_refs: Research F PI004/PI005/PI016; VISION personal-system reading
guardrail: Mark owner/contact as official, user-entered, inferred, or unknown; never invent ownership.

possible_failure_mode: Institution gets aggregate or individual access outside user intent.
evidence_refs: VISION.md cross-patient comparison guardrail; WellBe M02
guardrail: User-only consent gates; revocable share grants; no default population analytics.

possible_failure_mode: Model uses stigmatizing labels, demographics, or incomplete records to minimize or over-escalate concerns.
evidence_refs: Research F PI014/PI019; Research E cultural safety; Research B mental-health misattribution
guardrail: Bias controls, sensitive-field minimization, user correction, and no ethnicity-based scoring.

possible_failure_mode: Clinician packet becomes too long or wearable logs create noise.
evidence_refs: Research D clinician overload; SHM F012/R003
guardrail: One-page packet default; trends over raw streams; source-linked drill-down.

possible_failure_mode: Uncertainty, abnormal results, or red flags are phrased in a way that increases anxiety without a path.
evidence_refs: Research F safety principles; WellBe safety levels
guardrail: Plain language; action path; no alarm without next step; calibrated urgency levels.

risk_name: Unverified patient-held data treated as verified
possible_failure_mode: Photos, PDFs, or manual entries are treated as equivalent to official results.
evidence_refs: Research F PI018; SHM patient records facts
guardrail: Verification status and source quality displayed everywhere.

possible_failure_mode: Tool asks CHWs/clinics to do tasks without supplies, referral backup, or supervision.
evidence_refs: Research F PI020/PI021; Research E low-resource risks; SHM F017
guardrail: Only deploy with local protocol, supervision, offline mode, and referral capacity mapping.

possible_failure_mode: Feature crosses from memory/education into regulated clinical decision support without controls.
evidence_refs: SHM F015; Research F safety areas
guardrail: Feature classification review before release; keep user-facing outputs explainable and non-diagnostic.

possible_failure_mode: Health Thread value depends on long-term use, but users may stop logging before memory compounds.
guardrail: Low-friction capture, reminders tied to open loops, import-first value, and visit-triggered workflows.
